name: Margert Community Corporation
description_short: Margert Community Corporation is a NYC-based nonprofit providing free housing counseling, homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and energy efficiency assistance to low-to-moderate income households in Queens.
description_long:
Margert Community Corporation is a community-based nonprofit organization headquartered in Far Rockaway, Queens (relocated to 10-18A Beach 20th Street as of November 2024). The organization focuses on neighborhood preservation and energy affordability, serving primarily low-to-moderate income (LMI) households in South Queens and surrounding areas. Margert operates as an officially designated HUD Housing Counseling Agency and a New York State Preservation Company, indicating formal government recognition and certification.

Margert offers a comprehensive suite of free services centered on housing stability and energy efficiency. These include HUD-approved housing counseling for renters and homeowners, homebuyer education seminars, foreclosure prevention assistance through mortgage relief navigation, pre-purchase housing counseling, NYC Affordable Housing Lottery application support through Housing Ambassadors, and energy efficiency improvements via the Weatherization Assistance Program. The organization also administers the RESTORE Senior Homeowner Repairs program for residents age 60+, providing financial resources for emergency repairs and code violations. All core counseling and informational services appear to be offered at no charge to eligible clients.

Margert distinguishes itself through multiple governmental partnerships and certifications that validate service quality. As a HUD-approved housing counselor network member, a New York State Preservation Company, a Weatherization Assistance Program provider, and a NYSERDA contractor, the organization operates under established standards and compliance frameworks. The organization actively connects clients to broader government benefit programs (ACCESS HRA, ACCESS NYC, Benefits.gov, ConnectHomeUSA) rather than attempting to solve problems in isolation, positioning itself as a bridge to comprehensive social services.

The primary limitation is that Margert's service delivery model depends heavily on volunteer staff (particularly Housing Ambassadors) and government funding streams, which may create variability in availability and responsiveness. The organization does not provide direct financial assistance or loans—it exclusively offers counseling, education, and navigation services. Clients seeking immediate cash assistance, credit repair, or debt relief would need to be referred elsewhere. Geographic service appears concentrated in South Queens, though the scope of their Weatherization program extends across a broader South Queens region.
pros: HUD-approved Housing Counseling Agency with formal government certification and oversight
Free services across multiple housing and energy domains with no upfront fees
Operates as New York State Preservation Company with statutory authority to provide rehabilitation counseling
Provides specialized RESTORE program grants for seniors age 60+ addressing emergency home repairs
Active partnership with NYC HPD as Housing Ambassadors for affordable housing lottery navigation
Weatherization Assistance Program provider reducing heating/cooling costs for income-eligible households
Connects clients to broader government programs (HEAP, SNAP, Medicaid) via ACCESS HRA and Benefits.gov
cons: Service availability depends heavily on volunteer staffing, creating potential inconsistency and wait times
Geographic service primarily limited to South Queens/Far Rockaway area despite some program extensions
Does not provide direct financial assistance, loans, or debt relief—only counseling and navigation
No indication of multilingual staff or services despite serving diverse NYC populations
No online booking system or clear response time standards mentioned for client intake
best_for: Low-to-moderate income homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking pre-purchase education in South Queens
Senior homeowners (60+) needing emergency repair assistance and health/safety code violation remediation
NYC residents applying for affordable housing lottery programs seeking application guidance and preparation
Income-eligible families seeking energy efficiency improvements and reduced heating/cooling costs
diagnosis: Margert is best for low-income NYC residents (especially seniors and South Queens residents) seeking free, government-certified housing counseling, foreclosure prevention, energy efficiency assistance, or affordable housing navigation. The critical caveat is that Margert provides counseling and navigation only—not direct financial products, loans, or immediate cash assistance—and relies on volunteer staffing and government funding that may affect availability.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling for homeowners and renters
Foreclosure prevention and mortgage assistance relief navigation
NYC Affordable Housing Lottery application support via Housing Ambassadors
Weatherization Assistance Program energy efficiency measures and home improvements
RESTORE Senior Homeowner Repairs program for homeowners 60+
Tenant rights counseling and advocacy support
Community renewal and neighborhood preservation activities
Referrals to government benefits programs (HEAP, SNAP, Medicaid, Fair Fares NYC)
Energy affordability consultation and NYSERDA contractor services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
